weapons.name: Siluro a Lenta Corsa (SLC) 'Maiale'
weapons.description: Italy's most famous special weapon. Two frogmen rode astride a slow-running torpedo to enemy harbours, detached the warhead, and set a timer before making their escape. In December 1941, operators from the 10th MAS Flotilla sank HMS Queen Elizabeth and HMS Valiant at Alexandria, immobilising the British Mediterranean Battle Fleet.
weapons.operational_use: Alexandria (1941), Gibraltar (multiple raids), other Mediterranean ports

weapons.name: Barchina Esplosiva (MTM)
weapons.description: A small plywood motorboat packed with 300kg of explosive. The pilot aimed at a target, locked the wheel, and bailed out before impact. Used in the raid on Souda Bay (1941) that damaged HMS York.

weapons.name: Gamma class frogman suit
weapons.description: Italy pioneered combat diving with the Gamma Group (10th MAS). Operators wore rubberised suits, closed-circuit oxygen rebreathers, and fins — equipment that was revolutionary for the era and influenced post-war special forces globally.
